questions.question: In the context of estimating the mean download speed for a new broadband package, a sample of 50 connections yields a mean of 85 Mbps with a known population standard deviation of 10 Mbps. Construct a 95% confidence interval for the true mean download speed. Show your calculation steps and interpret the result in a practical context.
questions.modelAnswer: The 95% confidence interval is (82.23, 87.77). This means that we are 95% confident that the true mean download speed for all connections of this package is between 82.23 and 87.77 Mbps.

questions.question: A developer claims their algorithm solves a specific problem in under 100ms on average. In a test of 30 runs, the mean time was 92ms with a sample standard deviation of 15ms. Construct a 99% confidence interval for the true mean solve time. Does this interval support the developer's claim? Justify your answer.
questions.modelAnswer: The 99% confidence interval is (84.45, 99.55). Since the entire interval is below 100ms, this provides strong statistical support for the developer's claim.

questions.question: Explain the precise meaning of the confidence level in a confidence interval. Provide an example to illustrate your explanation.
questions.modelAnswer: The confidence level is the probability that the confidence interval contains the true population parameter. For example, a 95% confidence interval for a mean indicates that if we were to take many samples and build an interval from each, we would expect 95% of those intervals to contain the true population mean.
